Agent Foundations & Tools.aiRubric: Check agentic concepts, function calling logic, MCP configuration, ReAct loop understanding, and API integration.

lessons.title: Intro to Agentic AI vs Traditional LLMs
lessons.theory:
## Agents vs LLMs
While LLMs just generate text, Agents use tools to take actions in the environment.
```python
# Traditional: model.predict("Hello")
# Agentic: agent.run("Check the weather and email me")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Agent Capability
Assign the string `'Actions'` to the variable describing what Agents can perform that base LLMs cannot.

lessons.title: LLM Tool Calling (Function Calling)
lessons.theory:
## Tool Calling
Models can be provided with a schema of functions to execute.
```python
@tool
def get_weather(location: str):
    return "Sunny"
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Define a Tool
Use the `@tool` decorator on the `search_web` function.

lessons.title: Introduction to the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
lessons.theory:
## MCP
MCP provides a standard interface for agents to access external context and tools securely.
```python
mcp_client = Client(server="filesystem")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: MCP Client
Instantiate an MCP Client connecting to the `'database'` server.

lessons.title: Building a Simple ReAct (Reason + Act) Agent
lessons.theory:
## ReAct Pattern
Agents loop through: Thought, Action, Observation, Answer.
```python
# Thought: I need to search.
# Action: search("MCP")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: ReAct Step
Assign `'Observation'` to the step following an Action.

lessons.title: Connecting Agents to External APIs
lessons.theory:
## API Integration
Agents use tools to fetch data from APIs to ground their responses.
```python
requests.get("https://api.weather.gov")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Fetch API Data
Write the Python code to perform a `GET` request on `'https://api.example.com'` using the `requests` library.

Stateful Agents & Memory.aiRubric: Check LangGraph concepts, memory implementations, checkpointing, human-in-the-loop logic, and research agent components.

lessons.title: Intro to LangGraph for Stateful Workflows
lessons.theory:
## LangGraph
A framework for building stateful, multi-actor applications with LLMs.
```python
builder = StateGraph(AgentState)
builder.add_node("reason", reason_node)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Add Node
Add a node named `'act'` mapping to the `action_node` function in the StateGraph.

lessons.title: Implementing Short-Term & Long-Term Memory
lessons.theory:
## Memory
Short-term memory holds the current conversation. Long-term memory uses vector databases.
```python
memory.save_context({"input": "Hi"}, {"output": "Hello"})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Vector DB Type
Assign `'Vector'` to the type of database typically used for Long-Term semantic memory.

lessons.title: Managing Agent State & Checkpointing
lessons.theory:
## Checkpointing
Saves the agent's graph state so workflows can be paused and resumed.
```python
checkpointer = MemorySaver()
graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Compile Graph
Compile the `builder` passing `checkpointer` as an argument.

lessons.title: Human-in-the-Loop (Approval Workflows)
lessons.theory:
## Human-in-the-Loop
Agents pause execution to wait for human approval before dangerous actions.
```python
graph.invoke(state, config={"interrupt_before": ["execute"]})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Interrupt Node
Configure the graph invocation to interrupt before the `'deploy'` node.
lessons.rubric: Graph configured to interrupt before deploy.

lessons.title: Building a Web-Searching Research Agent
lessons.theory:
## Research Agent
A specific agent designed to issue queries, scrape pages, and summarize findings.
```python
@tool
def scrape_page(url: str):
    # logic here
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Research Steps
Assign `'Summarize'` as the final step after querying and scraping.

Multi-Agent Orchestration.aiRubric: Check multi-agent network topologies, delegation mechanisms, evaluation harnesses, infinite loop prevention, and deployment concepts.

lessons.title: Multi-Agent Architectures (Hierarchical vs Network)
lessons.theory:
## Topologies
Hierarchical uses a Supervisor agent. Network allows peer-to-peer agent communication.
```python
supervisor.delegate(task, worker_agent)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Supervisor Role
Assign `'Supervisor'` to the architecture where one agent manages others.

lessons.title: Agent Collaboration & Task Delegation
lessons.theory:
## Delegation
Agents pass context and tools to specialized sub-agents.
```python
sub_agent = create_agent(tools=[search])
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Create Sub-Agent
Create a `coder` agent equipped with the `write_code` tool.

lessons.title: Harness Engineering for Agent Evaluation
lessons.theory:
## Evaluation
Agents require automated testing harnesses to evaluate their trajectories and tool usage, not just their final text output.
```python
assert "search_web" in trajectory.tool_calls
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Evaluate Trajectory
Write an assertion checking if `'read_file'` is in `trajectory.tools`.

lessons.title: Handling Agent Failures & Infinite Loops
lessons.theory:
## Max Steps
To prevent infinite ReAct loops, always define a maximum step limit.
```python
graph.invoke(state, {"recursion_limit": 10})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Recursion Limit
Invoke the graph with a `recursion_limit` of `5`.

lessons.title: Deploying Autonomous Agents to Production
lessons.theory:
## Deployment
Agents in production need streaming, concurrency, and robust error handling. FastAPI and WebSockets are common.
```python
@app.websocket("/ws")
async def agent_stream(ws):
    await ws.send_text(agent_output)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: WebSocket Endpoint
Define a FastAPI websocket endpoint at `"/agent"`.
